LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROP 65 SETTLEMENT AND PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT PERMIT THE COURT TO MAKE FINDINGS THAT THE STATUTE ALLOWS IT TO MAKE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THE REFORMULATION AND WARNING PROVISIONS ARE REASONABLE AND COMPLIANT WITH THE LAW. THE FEES AND PENALTY PROVISIONS ARE REASONABLE. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Apporve Propostion 65 Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THE REFORMULATION AND WARNING PROVISIONS ARE REASONABLE AND COMPLIANT WITH THE LAW. THE FEES AND PENALTY PROVISIONS ARE REASONABLE. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 = (301/PJB)
